question_text: Let 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛 be a random sample from a uniform distribution over the interval 2 , 2), where 𝜃> 0 is an unknown parameter. Then which of the following options is/are correct?

options.A: 2 max{𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛} is the maximum likelihood estimator of 𝜃
options.B: (min{𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛}, max{𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛}) is a sufficient statistic
options.C: (min{𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛}, max{𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛}) is a complete statistic
options.D: 2(𝑛+1) max{|𝑋1|, … , |𝑋𝑛|} is a uniformly minimum variance unbiased estimator of 𝜃
